closed_reason: PR review fix: harden store lock acquire/update/release/remove against stale-handle path races
description: Store lock acquisition/reclaim relies on PID checks vulnerable to PID reuse and ownership ambiguity. Use lease token + epoch + freshness invariants so lock ownership is explicit, monotonic, and unforgeable by PID reuse.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs @ 89f68253 (change rztpznwr).

Implemented lease-epoch store lock ownership with per-acquire lease tokens, owner-matched heartbeat/release/drop safety, and offline unlock decisions that combine PID state with lease freshness instead of trusting PID identity alone.

Proof and review:
- Einstein adversarial correctness review: clean after iteration
- Huygens adversarial proof/tests review: clean after iteration
- Added regressions for stale-owner overwrite/delete rejection, offline unlock replacement-race safety, and legacy lock compatibility across reclaim and offline unlock paths.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all --check
- just dylint
- cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- cargo xtest
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit follow-up: PR review uncovered one real remaining gap in the lease-epoch unlock model. The offline unlock path still removed locks when PID inspection returned `Missing` even if the lease heartbeat was still fresh, which bypassed the freshness proof the bead was supposed to enforce.

Follow-up fix: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs @ 4d48550a (change zypzxorp).
- `OfflinePidState::Missing` now requires either an expired lease or explicit `--force` before unlock removes the lock.
- Added regression `offline_unlock_requires_force_for_fresh_missing_pid_lock`.
- Updated the stale-lock regression so the non-forced remove path still proves the expired-lease case rather than a fresh heartbeat.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all --check
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --lib offline_unlock_ -- --nocapture
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness
- cargo clippy -p beads-daemon --all-features -- -D warnings
- just dylint

Review:
- Huygens adversarial proof/tests review: clean
- Einstein review raised a dead-local-pid convenience tradeoff, but not a correctness bug; kept the stricter behavior because this bead's intent is to combine PID state with lease freshness instead of trusting PID checks alone.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
Audit follow-up: PR review found three remaining lock-path race windows in the lease-epoch model, and this change closes them on the final open bead state.

Follow-up fix: /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs @ 51706af5 (change qvoymwnt).
- Store-lock acquire/update/release/remove now serialize same-process path mutations with a striped path guard and serialize cross-process ownership transitions by taking an exclusive lock on the current lock file.
- Current-file ownership now validates against the live pathname after locking, and heartbeat/release/reclaim each revalidate again immediately before rewrite/unlink so stale handles cannot clobber or delete a replacement after owner match.
- Added direct regressions for reclaim/release/heartbeat path swaps after owner validation, plus deterministic same-process interleaving regressions for stale release and stale heartbeat races.

Verification:
- cargo fmt --all --check
- just dylint
- cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test
- cargo xtest
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness

Review:
- Einstein adversarial correctness review: clean after iteration; found one real reclaim-path proof gap that is now fixed
- Huygens adversarial proof/tests review: clean after iteration; found one real path-swap proof gap that is now fixed

title: P0: Replace PID-only lock authority with lease-epoch ownership model
